query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Evasion', 'The act of eluding or avoiding, particularly some pressure', 'Her evasion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evasive', 'Tending to avoid making revelations about oneself', 'The author hoped the new policy would evasive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eventual', 'Happening at some indefinite future time', 'The essay highlights the eventual of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evert', 'To turn inside out', 'Critics argued that the plan might evert the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evict', 'To expel (one or more people) from their property', 'Over time, the community learned to evict its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evident', 'Clear; easily seen or understood', 'The passage presents a evident portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evidential', 'Of or providing evidence', 'Her evidential became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evince', 'To display or demonstrate something clearly or convincingly', 'Critics argued that the plan might evince the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evocation', 'An act or instance of evoking,  a calling forth', 'Her evocation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evoke', 'To cause a thing to manifest in someone''s mind', 'Over time, the community learned to evoke its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evolution', 'Any process of formation or growth, development', 'Her evolution became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evolve', 'To develop gradually', 'Her evolve became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ewe', 'A female sheep, especially when fully mature', 'The essay highlights the ewe of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exacerbate', 'To make a problem worse', '“His rude remarks exacerbated the tension in the room.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exact', 'Strictly accurate or correct', 'Her exact became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exactitude', 'Precision and accuracy in details', 'Her exactitude became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exaggerate', 'To describe something as larger or greater than it actually is', 'Over time, the community learned to exaggerate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exalt', 'Praise or elevate to a high position', 'Critics argued that the plan might exalt the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exasperate', 'To irritate or provoke to a high degree, annoy extremely', 'Critics argued that the plan might exasperate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exasperation', 'To irritate or provoke to a high degree, annoy extremely', 'Her exasperation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excavate', 'To make a hole in (something), to hollow', 'Over time, the community learned to excavate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exce', 'To go beyond a limit or expectation', '“Her performance exceeded all expectations.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excel', 'To be better or do better than someone or something', 'Critics argued that the plan might excel the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excellence', 'The state of possessing good qualities in an eminent degree', 'Her excellence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exceptionable', 'Liable to exception  or objection, objectionable', 'What seemed exceptionable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excess', 'More than is normal, necessary or specified', 'The essay highlights the excess of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excitable', 'Easily excited', 'What seemed excitable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excitation', 'The act of exciting or putting in motion', 'Her excitation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exclamation', 'A loud calling or crying out, indicative of emotion', 'Scholars noted a striking exclamation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exclusion', 'The act of shutting out', 'Her exclusion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excoriate', 'To denounce or berate severely', 'The author hoped the new policy would excoriate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excoriation', 'The act of excoriating or flaying', 'The essay highlights the excoriation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excrescence', 'Something, usually abnormal, growing out of something else', 'Her excrescence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excretion', 'The process of removing material that has no further utility', 'Her excretion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excruciate', 'To inflict intense pain on (someone), to torture', 'The author hoped the new policy would excruciate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exculpate', 'To clear from blame or guilt', 'Critics argued that the plan might exculpate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excursion', 'A brief recreational trip, a journey out of the usual way', 'Scholars noted a striking excursion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Excusable', 'Possible to excuse', 'Over time, the community learned to excusable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Execrable', 'Utterly detestable, abominable, abhorrent', 'What seemed execrable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Execration', 'A curse dictated by violent feelings of hatred', 'The essay highlights the execration of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Executor', 'A person who performs some duty, job, etc', 'Scholars noted a striking executor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exegesis', 'Critical explanation or interpretation of a text', 'Scholars noted a striking exegesis between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exemplar', 'Something fit to be imitated, ideal or model', 'Critics argued that the plan might exemplar the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exemplary', 'Serving as a desirable model', 'The reviewer described the tone as exemplary, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exemplify', 'To serve as an example of something', '“His dedication exemplifies true leadership.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exempt', 'To grant (someone) freedom or immunity from', 'Critics argued that the plan might exempt the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exert', 'To put in vigorous action', 'Critics argued that the plan might exert the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exhale', 'To expel air from the lungs', 'Over time, the community learned to exhale its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exhaustive', 'Including or considering all elements or aspects; thorough', 'The reviewer described the tone as exhaustive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exhilarate', 'To enliven, invigorate, stimulate', 'Critics argued that the plan might exhilarate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
